profile.name: Debanjan Biswas
profile.tagline: Quantizing coordinate states. Developing mechanics, solving advanced calculations, and mapping physical systems from the molecular to the cosmic scale.

books.title: Pathfinder for Olympiad & JEE Advanced
books.author: Arvind Tiwari, Sachin Singh
books.description: Advanced problem arrays targeting deep mathematical application, multi-concept mechanics, and electromagnetic vectors.

books.title: Problems in General Physics
books.author: I.E. Irodov
books.description: Classic benchmark problem set covering general mechanics, thermodynamics, electrodynamics, and molecular physics calculations.

books.title: Problems in Physics
books.author: S.S. Krotov
books.description: High-level intellectual challenges from Russian Physics Olympiads focusing on elegant symmetry, mechanical balances, and electromagnetic induction.

books.title: Fundamentals of Physics
books.author: Resnick, Halliday, Walker
books.description: Essential conceptual and mathematical foundation for rigorous physical theory, wave mechanics, and special relativity.

domains.name: Classical Mechanics
domains.description: Formulating rigid body dynamics, non-inertial frames, Lagrangian approaches, rotational systems, planetary mechanics, and chaotic double pendulums.

domains.name: Electrodynamics
domains.description: Calculating electric potentials, boundary conditions, Gauss's law vector integration, capacitor dielectric networks, and magnetic Lorentz currents.

domains.name: Thermodynamics & KTG
domains.description: Solving thermodynamic cycles, entropy distributions, gas equations of state, Maxwell-Boltzmann velocity curves, and heat transmission coefficients.

domains.name: Optics & Waves
domains.description: Interference physics, Young's double slit intensity profiles, thin film diffraction, polarizations, acoustic Doppler shifts, and standing wave resonance.

domains.name: Modern & Quantum Physics
domains.description: Investigating wave-particle dualities, photoelectric voltage thresholds, Bohr atomic orbitals, X-ray emission spectra, and special relativistic transformations.
